description.task: Implement Fibonacci using plain recursion and memoized recursion. Count function calls made by each approach to demonstrate the exponential vs polynomial improvement. Use a HashMap as the memo cache.
instructions: long res = fibMemo(n-1,memo) + fibMemo(n-2,memo); memo.put(n,res); return res;
Recursive calls for fib(20): 21891; Memo calls: 39
description.hint: if(memo.containsKey(n))return memo.get(n); long res=fibMemo(n-1,memo)+fibMemo(n-2,memo); memo.put(n,res); return res;
description.expectedOutput:
fib(20) recursive: 6765, calls: 21891
fib(20) memo:      6765, calls: 39
Speedup factor: 561

description.task: Count the number of distinct ways to climb n stairs if you can take 1 or 2 steps at a time. Solve it three ways: (1) naive recursion, (2) memoization, (3) bottom-up tabulation. Show all three produce the same result.
instructions: ClimbingStairs is equivalent to Fibonacci: ways(n)=fib(n+1)
All three approaches must return identical results
description.expectedOutput:
n= 1: recursive=1, memo=1, tab=1, same=true
n= 4: recursive=5, memo=5, tab=5, same=true
n= 5: recursive=8, memo=8, tab=8, same=true
n=10: recursive=89, memo=89, tab=89, same=true

description.task: Demonstrate optimal substructure by solving the Minimum Cost Path in a grid. At each cell, you can move right or down. The minimum cost to reach the bottom-right corner can be broken into minimum cost subproblems at each cell.
instructions: Inner loops: for(int i=1;i<m;i++) for(int j=1;j<n;j++) dp[i][j]=grid[i][j]+Math.min(dp[i-1][j],dp[i][j-1]);
DP avoids recomputing subpaths — each cell solved once in O(m*n)
Optimal substructure: min cost to (i,j) = grid[i][j] + min(cost to (i-1,j), cost to (i,j-1))
